name: Gerald Wilson
abstract: Gerald Stanley Wilson (September 4, 1918 – September 8, 2014) was an American jazz trumpeter, big band bandleader, composer, arranger, and educator. Born in Mississippi, he was based in Los Angeles from the early 1940s.

interviews.title: Snooky Young and Gerald Wilson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995
Gerald Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: Gerald is always easy to work with so we talked about the music—it was not any, not any problem—and figured out what to do and so I had stuff with the big band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Collette
about_quotes.evidence: Gerald Wilson playing trumpet, Red Callender on the bass, Larry Bunker, I had a lot of people. ... These were all great players that I knew
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy Collette interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: Gerald Wilson was making arrangements for us... Billy Strayhorn and Gerald Wilson and Dudley Brooks were making our arrangements.

about_quotes.speaker: Young Snooky
about_quotes.evidence: Chick Carter‘s band, I played with Chick Carter‘s band and Gerald got in, Gerald Wilson got in Chick Carter‘s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Young Snooky — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Wess
about_quotes.evidence: We’ve all known each other since 1940, you know what I mean? It’s like old friends, you get to know them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Wess — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Dodgion
about_quotes.evidence: I knew Jerome Richardson from San Francisco and we had played together in Gerald Wilson’s band there.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Dodgion interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996
